summary: Wellington Security was incorrectly matched to a PSPLA/license entry belonging to a different company, Security Alert Wellington.
raw: Wellington Security and Security Alert Wellington are two different companies.

summary: Hines Security was matched to an expired PSPLA license instead of the current active license under the same name.
raw: Hines electrical do have a PSPLA license. you have picked the old one which is expired. there is a new one active under the same name. you need to always check for others before thinking the expired/not active license is the one.

summary: Livewire Electrical was incorrectly matched to Addz Livewire on PSPLA when they are separate companies.
raw: Livewire Electrical is not Addz Livewire like you've found on PSPLA, and its not Sefton Electrical either like you've found on the NZSA Membership. Please take a look and see why you choose the wrong companies.

summary: Livewire Electrical was incorrectly linked to PSPLA entry 'Addz Livewire Electrical' which is a different company, and was also incorrectly matched to Sefton Electrical from NZSA membership despite having a different email domain.
raw: THis company is called Livewire Electrical, the website is https://livewireelectrical.co.nz. You then linked them to PSPLA for "Addz Livewire Electrical" a totally different company in the companies office. You correctly linked Livewire Electrical in this record but then picked the PSPLA that links back to a different business in the companies office? You then picked "Sefton Electrical" as a match from the NZSA membership and their email ends in @seftonelectrical.co.nz, nowhere near the same domain as Livewire Electrical which you clearly have as @livewireelectrical.co.nz.

summary: The database entry is linking to an incorrect Facebook page belonging to a different American company rather than the actual company's social media presence.
raw: This is linking an american company for the facebook page. I am guessing you can't get much from facebook to help avoid this?
